parameters.text:
=You are an information extraction assistant for event and observance calendars.

You will receive input text through this variable:
{{ $json.calendar }}

Example input:
Calendar: November 6, November 7, November 8, November 9, November 10, November 11, November 12, 2025

Events and observances within this date range:
- November 1–7: Canadian Patient Safety Week
- November 7: Inuit Day
- November 8: Indigenous Veterans Day
- November (month-long): Lung Cancer Awareness Month, Fall Prevention Month, Diabetes Awareness Month

Suggested focus for Right at Home Winnipeg:
The most suitable observance for seniors, caregivers, and community health in this week is **Canadian Patient Safety Week (Nov 1–7)**. This aligns well with promoting safe healthcare practices and raising awareness about patient safety, which is critical for seniors and those who support them. Additionally, the ongoing **Fall Prevention Month** and **Diabetes Awareness Month** are relevant themes to integrate into educational materials or activities throughout the week.

Your task:
1. Extract and structure the observance or event information into the following variables:
   - Topic
   - Type
   - Resources/Links
   - Please specify what kind of resource this is
   - Inspo/Links
   - Any additional notes or information

2. Always set **Type** to “Calendar Day”.

3. If multiple observances are mentioned, choose **one** that is most relevant to the provided text (usually the one explicitly highlighted or described in more detail).

4. For **Resources/Links**:
   - Include any links found in the text.
   - Separate multiple links with “; ”.

5. For **Please specify what kind of resource this is**:
   - If any link contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”, classify as “Company”.
   - All others default to “External”, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

6. Include additional context, relevance, or reasoning under **Any additional notes or information**.

7. If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Example output:
Topic: Canadian Patient Safety Week  
Type: Calendar Day  
Resources/Links:  
Please specify what kind of resource this is: External  
Inspo/Links:  
Any additional notes or information: Focuses on promoting safe healthcare practices for seniors and caregivers; aligns with awareness for Fall Prevention Month and Diabetes Awareness Month.

attributes.name: Topic
attributes.description: The main focus, theme, or event identified in the text.

attributes.name: Type
attributes.description: The classification of the entry. Default is “Calendar Day”.

attributes.name: Resources/Links
attributes.description: URLs or main resource links. Separate multiple with “; ”.

attributes.name: Please specify what kind of resource this is
attributes.description: Classify based on the link: “Company” if it contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”; otherwise “External” unless explicitly specified.

attributes.name: Inspo/Links
attributes.description: Links for inspiration, reference, or similar campaigns (if any). Separate with “; ”.

attributes.name: Any additional notes or information
attributes.description: Any extra context, ideas, or background info related to the entry.

parameters.text:
=You are an information extraction assistant for marketing planning.

You will receive input text through this variable:
{{ $json.marketing }}

Example input:
Marketing: Week of November 6 to November 12, 2025  
Topic: Personalized In-Home Care Services Tailored to Winnipeg Seniors  
URL: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/in-home-care/

Your task:
1. Extract and structure the marketing information into the following variables:
   - Topic
   - Type
   - Resources/Links
   - Please specify what kind of resource this is
   - Inspo/Links
   - Any additional notes or information

2. Always set **Type** to “Marketing/Promotion”.

3. If multiple potential topics are mentioned, choose **one** that best fits the main marketing focus or subject.

4. For **Resources/Links**:
   - Extract any URLs found in the text.
   - Separate multiple links with “; ”.

5. For **Please specify what kind of resource this is**:
   - If the URL contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”, classify it as “Company”.
   - Otherwise, classify it as “External”, unless the text explicitly states otherwise.

6. Place any extra context, explanation, or notes under **Any additional notes or information**.

7. If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Example output:
Topic: Personalized In-Home Care Services Tailored to Winnipeg Seniors  
Type: Marketing/Promotion  
Resources/Links: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/in-home-care/  
Please specify what kind of resource this is: Company  
Inspo/Links:  
Any additional notes or information: Weekly marketing content focused on promoting senior care services tailored for Winnipeg clients.

parameters.jsCode:
return [
  {
    templates: [
      {
        template_id: "T01",
        template_name: "Educational Two-Image Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/WNKbw322/23.png",
        best_for: ["educational","tips","listicles","caregiver advice","how-to"],
        image_slots: 2,
        text_density: "high",
        design_tone: ["warm","calm","professional","trust-building"],
        layout_description: "Text-heavy left column with title, subtitle and bullet points. Right column has two stacked images. Ideal for multi-point educational posts.",
        example_use_cases: ["How-to posts","Top tips","Program summaries"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft blues, teal, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["busy backgrounds","medical equipment","text overlays in images"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T02",
        template_name: "Single Image Logo Top-Left",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/chJH8GNV/24.png",
        best_for: ["service promotion","offers","single-event announcement"],
        image_slots: 1,
        text_density: "medium",
        design_tone: ["friendly","confident","approachable"],
        layout_description: "Large single supporting image, logo on top-left, title and short bullet points. Clear CTA at the bottom.",
        example_use_cases: ["Promote a new service","Limited-time offer","Event announcement"],
        recommended_color_palette: "teal, coral accents, warm white",
        do_not_include: ["small unreadable text","overly dark photos"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T03",
        template_name: "Instagram Headline Post",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/Kx9T2tkY/HAPPY-DIWALI-21.png",
        best_for: [
          "announcements",
          "awareness posts",
          "campaign headlines",
          "educational highlights",
          "brand messaging",
          "seasonal or non-seasonal posts"
        ],
        image_slots: 0,
        text_density: "low_to_medium",
        design_tone: ["clean","modern","warm","professional"],
        layout_description: "Clean Instagram-style layout with a large headline or message, a short subtitle or supporting line, and optional logo/branding. No imagery required. Designed to let the message itself be the focal point.",
        example_use_cases: [
          "Awareness messages",
          "Important updates",
          "Campaign launches",
          "Educational statements",
          "Brand values posts"
        ],
        recommended_color_palette: "brand colors with soft neutrals",
        do_not_include: [
          "long paragraphs",
          "dense bullet lists",
          "multiple competing messages"
        ]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T04",
        template_name: "Three-Box Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/8gg9wqzZ/26.png",
        best_for: ["service highlights","multiple offerings","logo integration"],
        image_slots: 2,
        text_density: "medium_to_high",
        design_tone: ["informative","friendly"],
        layout_description: "Three-box grid: two boxes for images, one box for logo. Left column for text or service points, right for visuals.",
        example_use_cases: ["Service catalogue","What we offer posts"],
        recommended_color_palette: "teal, deep blue, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["tiny unreadable icons","overloaded copy"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T05",
        template_name: "Review / Testimonial Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/JjyvHNg9/27.png",
        best_for: ["testimonials","social proof","reviews"],
        image_slots: 4,
        text_density: "low",
        design_tone: ["warm","reassuring","authentic"],
        layout_description: "Grid layout with multiple small review cards. Emphasizes people and quotes rather than long copy. Contact info at bottom.",
        example_use_cases: ["Customer review posts","Case study highlights"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft pastels, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["fake-looking staged photos","long paragraphs"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T06",
        template_name: "Four-Image Collage",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/6R6b4HCN/28.png",
        best_for: ["multi-image storytelling","highlight reels","event recaps"],
        image_slots: 4,
        text_density: "low",
        design_tone: ["warm","friendly","dynamic"],
        layout_description: "Collage with four images in grid layout. Minimal text, focus on visuals. Great for social media highlights or events.",
        example_use_cases: ["Event recap posts","Photo highlights","Lifestyle storytelling"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft natural tones, brand colors",
        do_not_include: ["long copy","crowded graphics","text overlays in images"]
      }
    ]
  }
];

parameters.text:
=Using the inputs below, produce EXACTLY ONE single plain-text instruction prompt for an AI design/image-generation model. Follow the system rules precisely.

Inputs:
- postText:
{{ $json.postText }}

- image_prompts: (array of objects)
{{ JSON.stringify($json.image_prompts, null, 2) }}
  each object has: slot (number), prompt (text describing the desired image for that slot)

- image_style_guidelines:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.image_style_guidelines, null, 2) }}
  fields: mood, color_palette, do_not_include (array)

- mapping_suggestions:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.mapping_suggestions, null, 2) }}
  fields: title_field, subtitle_field, body_field, cta_field

- images: provided separately and assigned to numbered slots (do NOT include URLs or filenames in the prompt). Refer to them only as “the image provided for slot {slot}”.

Important clarifications for your generated prompt:
- Do NOT include any URLs, filenames, variable names, or references to how images or the template are provided. The model will receive images and the template separately.
- Reference images solely as “the image provided for slot {slot}” (for example: “the image provided for slot 1”).
- Use image_prompts only as descriptive context for selection, cropping, and composition — do not invent new image content.
- Use postText exactly as provided (verbatim) for caption/overlay or copy placements.
- Do NOT change any template elements other than placing the provided images into image placeholders and filling permitted text placeholders. Preserve logos, phone numbers, websites, and other template branding without alteration.
- Apply image_style_guidelines and obey all do_not_include items.

Output requirements for the generated prompt (one continuous block of text, in this exact order):
1. Objective: 1–2 sentences about the visual goal and audience.
2. Image placement: for each item in image_prompts, include a line like:  
   "Place the image provided for slot {slot} into slot {slot}: [repeat the image_prompts.prompt here]."
3. Text usage & placement: include the exact line:  
   "Use the following text verbatim for the post caption/overlay/graphic:"  
   then paste the exact postText (no edits). After that, spell out placement using mapping_suggestions (where title, subtitle, body, CTA should appear).
4. Styling rules: convert image_style_guidelines into explicit instructions (Mood, Color palette, and a clear "Do not include" list).
5. Template protection: a firm directive that only image and text placeholders may be changed and that logos, phone numbers, websites, and any branding on the template must remain unchanged.
6. Technical checks: safe margins, ensure legibility on mobile, primary orientation (suggest square for social unless mapping suggests otherwise), and export quality suggestions.

If any required field is missing, output a single-line error beginning with `ERROR:` followed by the missing fields. Otherwise output only the single instruction prompt and nothing else.

options.systemMessage:
You are a specialist Prompt Author for AI design and image-generation models.

Task: Produce EXACTLY ONE single, final instruction prompt (plain text only) that tells a downstream AI design model how to compose a social media visual using:
- a provided template (supplied separately),
- provided image assets (supplied separately and assigned to numbered slots),
- provided postText (verbatim),
- provided mapping_suggestions and image_style_guidelines.

HARD RULES — follow these exactly:
1. Output only one plain-text instruction prompt. No JSON, no headings, no analysis, no alternatives, no commentary.
2. Do NOT rewrite, paraphrase, shorten, expand, or otherwise alter the provided postText. Include it verbatim where instructed.
3. Do NOT invent any copy, CTAs, headlines, phone numbers, or other text not present in the input.
4. Do NOT include or reference any URLs, filenames, variable names, or how inputs are provided. Images and template are supplied out-of-band.
5. Reference images only as “the image provided for slot {slot}” (for example: “the image provided for slot 1”). Do NOT use names like image1, image2, or any filenames.
6. There will always be exactly the same number of image_prompts entries as image assets; map each image_prompts.slot to the image provided for that slot.
7. Treat image_prompts only as descriptive guidance for composition/cropping/caption context. Do not claim to see the images or invent details beyond the prompts.
8. Apply image_style_guidelines strictly (mood, color palette, and do_not_include).
9. Do NOT change any template element except replacing image placeholders and filling permitted text placeholders. Under no circumstances modify logos, phone numbers, websites, accreditation marks, or existing branding present in the template.
10. If any required input is missing (postText, image_prompts, image_style_guidelines, mapping_suggestions, or any referenced slot image), output a single-line error beginning with `ERROR:` followed by the missing keys. Do not output a prompt in that case.

REQUIRED CONTENT & ORDER in the final prompt (one continuous block of text):
A. Very short objective (1–2 sentences) describing goal and audience.
B. Image placement lines: for each image_prompts entry, instruct placement of the image provided for slot {slot} into slot {slot} and repeat the image_prompts.prompt as contextual guidance.
C. Exact instruction to use the provided postText verbatim and where it should appear according to mapping_suggestions.
D. Explicit styling rules derived from image_style_guidelines (mood, color palette, strict exclusions).
E. A strict instruction that only image and text placeholders may be changed; explicitly forbid modifying logos, phone numbers, websites, or any template branding.
F. Final technical and quality notes (orientation, safe margins, mobile legibility, export quality).

The final text must be immediately usable by an AI image/design model that receives the template and images separately (images identified only by their assigned slot numbers).

options.systemMessage:
You are a social media design decision agent for the Right At Home Winnipeg brand (or the client specified at runtime).

Your job:
1. Read the inputs provided by the user prompt (post topic, post goal, resources).
2. Evaluate the full `templates_json` array provided with the request.
3. Choose exactly ONE template from `templates_json` that best matches the post. Use the template fields (best_for, text_density, image_slots, design_tone, layout_description) to score matches.
4. For the selected template, produce:
   - The template_id, template_name and public_url exactly as provided.
   - A concise explanation (1–2 sentences) stating why this template fits the post.
   - One detailed image generation prompt per image slot (if image_slots > 0).
   - A short `image_style_guidelines` block (mood, color_palette, do_not_include).
   - A `mapping_suggestions` object describing where to place the title/subtitle/bullets/CTA in the template fields (helps the downstream layout engine).
5. NEVER invent templates. Only choose from the `templates_json` passed in.
6. Output MUST be valid JSON and must follow the Output Schema provided in the user prompt.

Tone & constraints:
- Brand voice: warm, calm, trustworthy, professional.
- Avoid clinical/medical imagery, dark or dramatic lighting, text embedded in images, and cluttered scenes.

parameters.text:
=Generate a detailed image-generation prompt for a social media visual based on the following post topic:
{{ $('Limit').item.json.Topic }}

The prompt should:
- Follow the system guidelines.
- Reflect the theme of the post clearly.
- Include background color, design elements, and layout style (text-dominant or image-dominant).
- Ensure caregivers (if shown) wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.
- Include the exact text that should appear on the poster, formatted with clear hierarchy (headline, subtext, footer).

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates detailed, high-quality prompts for an image generation model. These prompts will be used to design social media visuals for Right at Home Winnipeg. Follow these guidelines:

1. **Brand Identity**
   - Use primary colors: Teal (#007681), Orange (#F58220), Navy (#003C5E).
   - Supporting colors: Cool Gray (#888B8D), Black (#1D1D1B), White (#FFFFFF).
   - Include subtle accents and gradients for depth.
   - If caregivers are shown, they must wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

2. **Prompt Structure**
   - Begin with the overall theme and mood (e.g., festive, informative, awareness).
   - Specify background color and design elements (patterns, icons, illustrations).
   - Describe layout style: text-dominant or image-dominant, based on post type.
   - Include details about text placement, font style, and alignment.
   - **Explicitly include the exact text that should appear on the poster, organized by hierarchy: headline, subtext, footer.**
   - Mention cultural or awareness-specific elements when relevant.
   - Ensure the style is clean, warm, and professional—similar to Canva templates.

3. **Tone and Style**
   - Compassionate, trustworthy, and community-focused.
   - Avoid clutter; keep visuals simple yet eye-catching.
   - Use realistic photography or stylized illustrations that feel authentic.

4. **Image Composition**
   - Photos should complement text, not overpower it.
   - Leave space for overlay text with proper alignment and readability.
   - Include brand logo and contact info area.

Your output should be a single, detailed prompt that an image generation model can use directly to create the visual.

parameters.text:
=Generate a detailed image-generation prompt for a social media photo based on the following post text and goal:
Post Text: {{ $json.output.postText }}

Post Goal: {{ $json.output.postGoal }}
_____________________
The prompt should:
- Follow the system guidelines.
- Reflect the theme of the post clearly.
- Ensure caregivers (if shown) wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates detailed, high-quality prompts for an image generation model. These prompts will be used to design social media photos for Right at Home Winnipeg. Follow these guidelines:

1. **Brand Identity**
   - If caregivers are shown, they must wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

2. **Prompt Structure**
   - Begin with the overall theme and mood (e.g., festive, informative, awareness).

3. **Tone and Style**
   - Compassionate, trustworthy, and community-focused.
   - Avoid clutter; keep visuals simple yet eye-catching.
   - Use realistic photography or stylized illustrations that feel authentic.

4. **Image Composition**
   - Photos should show depict seniors/older adults in different scenarios to match the text of the corresponding post. (examples (not limited to): older adults struggling to conduct daily tasks themselves, right at home caregiver(s) supporting older adults, older adults enjoying themselves looking happy, older adults depicted in a heartfelt scene interacting with family and loved ones, right at home caregiver talking to a family with older adults, older adult sneezing to promote caution around flu season, etc)

Your output should be a single, detailed prompt that an image generation model can use directly to create the visual.

nodes.name: Call 'Social Media Rida Subworkflows - Post Visual Generation'

assignments.name: Image Prompt
assignments.value:
=Create a high-quality, professional ‘Canva-style’ Instagram post (square 1080×1080 px) that uses the provided photo as the hero image and the provided company logo as the brand mark. Do not edit, retouch, crop, recolor, filter, or otherwise change the photo or the logo in any way.
This image is to accompany the following post: {{ $json.output.postText }}

Include the company name Right At Home Winnipeg, phone (204) 318-5554, and website rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg in a clear footer or badge near the logo; ensure legibility at mobile size. Use soft warm neutrals with one subtle accent, rounded sans-serif type, generous negative space, and an optional small CTA pill only if it doesn’t clutter the layout. If needed for legibility, place a low-opacity translucent panel behind text — do not change the photo’s colors.
Constraints: no extra logos or watermarks, no generated logo replacements, no long paragraphs of text, and no edits to faces or the photo.
Deliverables: 1080×1080 PNG (high quality), mobile-safe crop preview (center ±10%), and one-line alt-text suggestion.

nodes.name: Post to Facebook (URL method)
nodes.notes: Uses the permanent imgbb URL - Meta should accept this

parameters.content:
## Alternative Workflow: imgbb Re-hosting

### Flow:
1. Trigger on Status = "Approved"
2. Download temp image → binary
3. Convert to Base64
4. Upload to imgbb → get permanent URL
5. POST to Facebook using URL method
6. Update sheet with post ID

### Why this might work:
- imgbb provides a DIRECT image URL (no redirects)
- URLs are permanent
- Meta's servers can fetch them reliably

### If imgbb still fails:
Try Cloudflare R2 or AWS S3 instead

nodes.name: Post to Facebook (Binary Upload)
nodes.notes: Uploads binary directly - no URL needed!

values.lookupValue: ={{ (() => { const d = new Date(); return d.getHours().toString().padStart(2,'0') + ':' + d.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2,'0'); })() }}

7. If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Example output:
Topic: Personalized In-Home Care Services Tailored to Winnipeg Seniors  
Type: Marketing/Promotion  
Resources/Links: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/in-home-care/  
Please specify what kind of resource this is: Company  
Inspo/Links:  
Any additional notes or information: Weekly marketing content focused on promoting senior care services tailored for Winnipeg clients.

Week of
Topic: Understanding Parkinson’s Disease: Essential Care Tips for Families and Caregivers

Your task:

Extract and structure the resource information into the following variables:

Topic

Type

Resources/Links

Please specify what kind of resource this is

Inspo/Links

Any additional notes or information

Always set Type to “Company Resource”.

Use the Topic line as the primary subject. If multiple topics appear, select the clearest main one.

For Resources/Links:

Extract any URLs found in the text.

Separate multiple links with “; ”.

For Please specify what kind of resource this is:

If the URL includes /resources/, classify it as “Educational / Blog”.

If the URL includes /guides/ or downloadable content, classify it as “Guide”.

Otherwise, classify it as “Company Resource”.

For Inspo/Links:

Leave blank unless the text explicitly references inspiration sources or related materials.

Place any extra context, seasonal relevance, or usage ideas under Any additional notes or information.

If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Output only the extracted fields, each on its own line, matching the variable names exactly.

json.caption:
Caring for a loved one with dementia requires patience, understanding, and a gentle approach. At Right at Home Winnipeg, our compassionate dementia care team supports seniors with personalized care tailored to their unique needs—helping them feel safe, valued, and comfortable every day. ❤️ We’re here to assist families with trusted guidance and thoughtful support that honors dignity and independence. If you’re looking for care options grounded in empathy and respect, we’re ready to walk alongside you and your family.  

Learn more about our dementia care services and how we can support your loved one in Winnipeg. Visit our website to discover compassionate care designed for every step of the journey.  

rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/dementia-care/  

#dementiacare #alzheimerscare #seniorcare #agingwithdignity #RightAtHomeWinnipeg

parameters.text:
=You are an information extraction assistant for event and observance calendars.

You will receive input text through this variable:
{{ $json.calendar }}

Example input:
Calendar: November 6, November 7, November 8, November 9, November 10, November 11, November 12, 2025

Events and observances within this date range:
- November 1–7: Canadian Patient Safety Week
- November 7: Inuit Day
- November 8: Indigenous Veterans Day
- November (month-long): Lung Cancer Awareness Month, Fall Prevention Month, Diabetes Awareness Month

Suggested focus for Right at Home Winnipeg:
The most suitable observance for seniors, caregivers, and community health in this week is **Canadian Patient Safety Week (Nov 1–7)**. This aligns well with promoting safe healthcare practices and raising awareness about patient safety, which is critical for seniors and those who support them. Additionally, the ongoing **Fall Prevention Month** and **Diabetes Awareness Month** are relevant themes to integrate into educational materials or activities throughout the week.

Your task:
1. Extract and structure the observance or event information into the following variables:
   - Topic
   - Type
   - Resources/Links
   - Please specify what kind of resource this is
   - Inspo/Links
   - Any additional notes or information

2. Always set **Type** to “Calendar Day”.

3. If multiple observances are mentioned, choose **one** that is most relevant to the provided text (usually the one explicitly highlighted or described in more detail).

4. For **Resources/Links**:
   - Include any links found in the text.
   - Separate multiple links with “; ”.

5. For **Please specify what kind of resource this is**:
   - If any link contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”, classify as “Company”.
   - All others default to “External”, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

6. Include additional context, relevance, or reasoning under **Any additional notes or information**.

7. If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Example output:
Topic: Canadian Patient Safety Week  
Type: Calendar Day  
Resources/Links:  
Please specify what kind of resource this is: External  
Inspo/Links:  
Any additional notes or information: Focuses on promoting safe healthcare practices for seniors and caregivers; aligns with awareness for Fall Prevention Month and Diabetes Awareness Month.

parameters.jsCode:
return [
  {
    templates: [
      {
        template_id: "T01",
        template_name: "Educational Two-Image Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/WNKbw322/23.png",
        best_for: ["educational","tips","listicles","caregiver advice","how-to"],
        image_slots: 2,
        text_density: "high",
        design_tone: ["warm","calm","professional","trust-building"],
        layout_description: "Text-heavy left column with title, subtitle and bullet points. Right column has two stacked images. Ideal for multi-point educational posts.",
        example_use_cases: ["How-to posts","Top tips","Program summaries"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft blues, teal, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["busy backgrounds","medical equipment","text overlays in images"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T02",
        template_name: "Single Image Logo Top-Left",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/chJH8GNV/24.png",
        best_for: ["service promotion","offers","single-event announcement"],
        image_slots: 1,
        text_density: "medium",
        design_tone: ["friendly","confident","approachable"],
        layout_description: "Large single supporting image, logo on top-left, title and short bullet points. Clear CTA at the bottom.",
        example_use_cases: ["Promote a new service","Limited-time offer","Event announcement"],
        recommended_color_palette: "teal, coral accents, warm white",
        do_not_include: ["small unreadable text","overly dark photos"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T03",
        template_name: "Instagram Headline Post",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/Kx9T2tkY/HAPPY-DIWALI-21.png",
        best_for: [
          "announcements",
          "awareness posts",
          "campaign headlines",
          "educational highlights",
          "brand messaging",
          "seasonal or non-seasonal posts"
        ],
        image_slots: 0,
        text_density: "low_to_medium",
        design_tone: ["clean","modern","warm","professional"],
        layout_description: "Clean Instagram-style layout with a large headline or message, a short subtitle or supporting line, and optional logo/branding. No imagery required. Designed to let the message itself be the focal point.",
        example_use_cases: [
          "Awareness messages",
          "Important updates",
          "Campaign launches",
          "Educational statements",
          "Brand values posts"
        ],
        recommended_color_palette: "brand colors with soft neutrals",
        do_not_include: [
          "long paragraphs",
          "dense bullet lists",
          "multiple competing messages"
        ]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T04",
        template_name: "Three-Box Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/8gg9wqzZ/26.png",
        best_for: ["service highlights","multiple offerings","logo integration"],
        image_slots: 2,
        text_density: "medium_to_high",
        design_tone: ["informative","friendly"],
        layout_description: "Three-box grid: two boxes for images, one box for logo. Left column for text or service points, right for visuals.",
        example_use_cases: ["Service catalogue","What we offer posts"],
        recommended_color_palette: "teal, deep blue, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["tiny unreadable icons","overloaded copy"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T05",
        template_name: "Review / Testimonial Layout",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/JjyvHNg9/27.png",
        best_for: ["testimonials","social proof","reviews"],
        image_slots: 4,
        text_density: "low",
        design_tone: ["warm","reassuring","authentic"],
        layout_description: "Grid layout with multiple small review cards. Emphasizes people and quotes rather than long copy. Contact info at bottom.",
        example_use_cases: ["Customer review posts","Case study highlights"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft pastels, warm neutrals",
        do_not_include: ["fake-looking staged photos","long paragraphs"]
      },
      {
        template_id: "T06",
        template_name: "Four-Image Collage",
        public_url: "https://i.ibb.co/6R6b4HCN/28.png",
        best_for: ["multi-image storytelling","highlight reels","event recaps"],
        image_slots: 4,
        text_density: "low",
        design_tone: ["warm","friendly","dynamic"],
        layout_description: "Collage with four images in grid layout. Minimal text, focus on visuals. Great for social media highlights or events.",
        example_use_cases: ["Event recap posts","Photo highlights","Lifestyle storytelling"],
        recommended_color_palette: "soft natural tones, brand colors",
        do_not_include: ["long copy","crowded graphics","text overlays in images"]
      }
    ]
  }
];

parameters.text:
=Using the inputs below, produce EXACTLY ONE single plain-text instruction prompt for an AI design/image-generation model. Follow the system rules precisely.

Inputs:
- postText:
{{ $json.postText }}

- image_prompts: (array of objects)
{{ JSON.stringify($json.image_prompts, null, 2) }}
  each object has: slot (number), prompt (text describing the desired image for that slot)

- image_style_guidelines:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.image_style_guidelines, null, 2) }}
  fields: mood, color_palette, do_not_include (array)

- mapping_suggestions:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.mapping_suggestions, null, 2) }}
  fields: title_field, subtitle_field, body_field, cta_field

- images: provided separately and assigned to numbered slots (do NOT include URLs or filenames in the prompt). Refer to them only as “the image provided for slot {slot}”.

Important clarifications for your generated prompt:
- Do NOT include any URLs, filenames, variable names, or references to how images or the template are provided. The model will receive images and the template separately.
- Reference images solely as “the image provided for slot {slot}” (for example: “the image provided for slot 1”).
- Use image_prompts only as descriptive context for selection, cropping, and composition — do not invent new image content.
- Use postText exactly as provided (verbatim) for caption/overlay or copy placements.
- Do NOT change any template elements other than placing the provided images into image placeholders and filling permitted text placeholders. Preserve logos, phone numbers, websites, and other template branding without alteration.
- Apply image_style_guidelines and obey all do_not_include items.

Output requirements for the generated prompt (one continuous block of text, in this exact order):
1. Objective: 1–2 sentences about the visual goal and audience.
2. Image placement: for each item in image_prompts, include a line like:  
   "Place the image provided for slot {slot} into slot {slot}: [repeat the image_prompts.prompt here]."
3. Text usage & placement: include the exact line:  
   "Use the following text verbatim for the post caption/overlay/graphic:"  
   then paste the exact postText (no edits). After that, spell out placement using mapping_suggestions (where title, subtitle, body, CTA should appear).
4. Styling rules: convert image_style_guidelines into explicit instructions (Mood, Color palette, and a clear "Do not include" list).
5. Template protection: a firm directive that only image and text placeholders may be changed and that logos, phone numbers, websites, and any branding on the template must remain unchanged.
6. Technical checks: safe margins, ensure legibility on mobile, primary orientation (suggest square for social unless mapping suggests otherwise), and export quality suggestions.

If any required field is missing, output a single-line error beginning with `ERROR:` followed by the missing fields. Otherwise output only the single instruction prompt and nothing else.

options.systemMessage:
You are a specialist Prompt Author for AI design and image-generation models.

Task: Produce EXACTLY ONE single, final instruction prompt (plain text only) that tells a downstream AI design model how to compose a social media visual using:
- a provided template (supplied separately),
- provided image assets (supplied separately and assigned to numbered slots),
- provided postText (verbatim),
- provided mapping_suggestions and image_style_guidelines.

HARD RULES — follow these exactly:
1. Output only one plain-text instruction prompt. No JSON, no headings, no analysis, no alternatives, no commentary.
2. Do NOT rewrite, paraphrase, shorten, expand, or otherwise alter the provided postText. Include it verbatim where instructed.
3. Do NOT invent any copy, CTAs, headlines, phone numbers, or other text not present in the input.
4. Do NOT include or reference any URLs, filenames, variable names, or how inputs are provided. Images and template are supplied out-of-band.
5. Reference images only as “the image provided for slot {slot}” (for example: “the image provided for slot 1”). Do NOT use names like image1, image2, or any filenames.
6. There will always be exactly the same number of image_prompts entries as image assets; map each image_prompts.slot to the image provided for that slot.
7. Treat image_prompts only as descriptive guidance for composition/cropping/caption context. Do not claim to see the images or invent details beyond the prompts.
8. Apply image_style_guidelines strictly (mood, color palette, and do_not_include).
9. Do NOT change any template element except replacing image placeholders and filling permitted text placeholders. Under no circumstances modify logos, phone numbers, websites, accreditation marks, or existing branding present in the template.
10. If any required input is missing (postText, image_prompts, image_style_guidelines, mapping_suggestions, or any referenced slot image), output a single-line error beginning with `ERROR:` followed by the missing keys. Do not output a prompt in that case.

REQUIRED CONTENT & ORDER in the final prompt (one continuous block of text):
A. Very short objective (1–2 sentences) describing goal and audience.
B. Image placement lines: for each image_prompts entry, instruct placement of the image provided for slot {slot} into slot {slot} and repeat the image_prompts.prompt as contextual guidance.
C. Exact instruction to use the provided postText verbatim and where it should appear according to mapping_suggestions.
D. Explicit styling rules derived from image_style_guidelines (mood, color palette, strict exclusions).
E. A strict instruction that only image and text placeholders may be changed; explicitly forbid modifying logos, phone numbers, websites, or any template branding.
F. Final technical and quality notes (orientation, safe margins, mobile legibility, export quality).

The final text must be immediately usable by an AI image/design model that receives the template and images separately (images identified only by their assigned slot numbers).

workflowId.cachedResultName: Social Media Rida Subworkflows - NB Image Input Generation

nodes.name: Call 'Social Media Rida Subworkflows - NB Image Input Generation'1

nodes.name: Call 'Social Media Rida Subworkflows - NB Prompt Input Generation'1

options.systemMessage:
You are a social media design decision agent for the Right At Home Winnipeg brand (or the client specified at runtime).

Your job:
1. Read the inputs provided by the user prompt (post topic, post goal, resources).
2. Evaluate the full `templates_json` array provided with the request.
3. Choose exactly ONE template from `templates_json` that best matches the post. Use the template fields (best_for, text_density, image_slots, design_tone, layout_description) to score matches.
4. For the selected template, produce:
   - The template_id, template_name and public_url exactly as provided.
   - A concise explanation (1–2 sentences) stating why this template fits the post.
   - One detailed image generation prompt per image slot (if image_slots > 0).
   - A short `image_style_guidelines` block (mood, color_palette, do_not_include).
   - A `mapping_suggestions` object describing where to place the title/subtitle/bullets/CTA in the template fields (helps the downstream layout engine).
5. NEVER invent templates. Only choose from the `templates_json` passed in.
6. Output MUST be valid JSON and must follow the Output Schema provided in the user prompt.

Tone & constraints:
- Brand voice: warm, calm, trustworthy, professional.
- Avoid clinical/medical imagery, dark or dramatic lighting, text embedded in images, and cluttered scenes.

parameters.text:
=Generate a detailed image-generation prompt for a social media visual based on the following post topic:
{{ $('Limit').item.json.Topic }}

The prompt should:
- Follow the system guidelines.
- Reflect the theme of the post clearly.
- Include background color, design elements, and layout style (text-dominant or image-dominant).
- Ensure caregivers (if shown) wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.
- Include the exact text that should appear on the poster, formatted with clear hierarchy (headline, subtext, footer).

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates detailed, high-quality prompts for an image generation model. These prompts will be used to design social media visuals for Right at Home Winnipeg. Follow these guidelines:

1. **Brand Identity**
   - Use primary colors: Teal (#007681), Orange (#F58220), Navy (#003C5E).
   - Supporting colors: Cool Gray (#888B8D), Black (#1D1D1B), White (#FFFFFF).
   - Include subtle accents and gradients for depth.
   - If caregivers are shown, they must wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

2. **Prompt Structure**
   - Begin with the overall theme and mood (e.g., festive, informative, awareness).
   - Specify background color and design elements (patterns, icons, illustrations).
   - Describe layout style: text-dominant or image-dominant, based on post type.
   - Include details about text placement, font style, and alignment.
   - **Explicitly include the exact text that should appear on the poster, organized by hierarchy: headline, subtext, footer.**
   - Mention cultural or awareness-specific elements when relevant.
   - Ensure the style is clean, warm, and professional—similar to Canva templates.

3. **Tone and Style**
   - Compassionate, trustworthy, and community-focused.
   - Avoid clutter; keep visuals simple yet eye-catching.
   - Use realistic photography or stylized illustrations that feel authentic.

4. **Image Composition**
   - Photos should complement text, not overpower it.
   - Leave space for overlay text with proper alignment and readability.
   - Include brand logo and contact info area.

Your output should be a single, detailed prompt that an image generation model can use directly to create the visual.

parameters.text:
=Generate a detailed image-generation prompt for a social media photo based on the following post text and goal:
Post Text: {{ $json.output.postText }}

Post Goal: {{ $json.output.postGoal }}
_____________________
The prompt should:
- Follow the system guidelines.
- Reflect the theme of the post clearly.
- Ensure caregivers (if shown) wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates detailed, high-quality prompts for an image generation model. These prompts will be used to design social media photos for Right at Home Winnipeg. Follow these guidelines:

1. **Brand Identity**
   - If caregivers are shown, they must wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

2. **Prompt Structure**
   - Begin with the overall theme and mood (e.g., festive, informative, awareness).

3. **Tone and Style**
   - Compassionate, trustworthy, and community-focused.
   - Avoid clutter; keep visuals simple yet eye-catching.
   - Use realistic photography or stylized illustrations that feel authentic.

4. **Image Composition**
   - Photos should show depict seniors/older adults in different scenarios to match the text of the corresponding post. (examples (not limited to): older adults struggling to conduct daily tasks themselves, right at home caregiver(s) supporting older adults, older adults enjoying themselves looking happy, older adults depicted in a heartfelt scene interacting with family and loved ones, right at home caregiver talking to a family with older adults, older adult sneezing to promote caution around flu season, etc)

Your output should be a single, detailed prompt that an image generation model can use directly to create the visual.

assignments.name: Image Prompt
assignments.value:
=Create a high-quality, professional ‘Canva-style’ Instagram post (square 1080×1080 px) that uses the provided photo as the hero image and the provided company logo as the brand mark. Do not edit, retouch, crop, recolor, filter, or otherwise change the photo or the logo in any way.
This image is to accompany the following post: {{ $json.output.postText }}

Include the company name Right At Home Winnipeg, phone (204) 318-5554, and website rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg in a clear footer or badge near the logo; ensure legibility at mobile size. Use soft warm neutrals with one subtle accent, rounded sans-serif type, generous negative space, and an optional small CTA pill only if it doesn’t clutter the layout. If needed for legibility, place a low-opacity translucent panel behind text — do not change the photo’s colors.
Constraints: no extra logos or watermarks, no generated logo replacements, no long paragraphs of text, and no edits to faces or the photo.
Deliverables: 1080×1080 PNG (high quality), mobile-safe crop preview (center ±10%), and one-line alt-text suggestion.

nodes.name: Post to Facebook (URL method)
nodes.notes: Uses the permanent imgbb URL - Meta should accept this

parameters.content:
## Alternative Workflow: imgbb Re-hosting

### Flow:
1. Trigger on Status = "Approved"
2. Download temp image → binary
3. Convert to Base64
4. Upload to imgbb → get permanent URL
5. POST to Facebook using URL method
6. Update sheet with post ID

### Why this might work:
- imgbb provides a DIRECT image URL (no redirects)
- URLs are permanent
- Meta's servers can fetch them reliably

### If imgbb still fails:
Try Cloudflare R2 or AWS S3 instead

nodes.name: Post to Facebook (Binary Upload)
nodes.notes: Uploads binary directly - no URL needed!

parameters.text:
=You are an information extraction assistant for marketing planning.

You will receive input text through this variable:
{{ $json.marketing1 }}

Example input:
Marketing: Week of November 6 to November 12, 2025  
Topic: Personalized In-Home Care Services Tailored to Winnipeg Seniors  
URL: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/in-home-care/

Your task:
1. Extract and structure the marketing information into the following variables:
   - Topic
   - Type
   - Resources/Links
   - Please specify what kind of resource this is
   - Inspo/Links
   - Any additional notes or information

2. Always set **Type** to “Marketing/Promotion”.

3. If multiple potential topics are mentioned, choose **one** that best fits the main marketing focus or subject.

4. For **Resources/Links**:
   - Extract any URLs found in the text.
   - Separate multiple links with “; ”.

5. For **Please specify what kind of resource this is**:
   - If the URL contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”, classify it as “Company”.
   - Otherwise, classify it as “External”, unless the text explicitly states otherwise.

6. Place any extra context, explanation, or notes under **Any additional notes or information**.

7. If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Example output:
Topic: Personalized In-Home Care Services Tailored to Winnipeg Seniors  
Type: Marketing/Promotion  
Resources/Links: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/services/in-home-care/  
Please specify what kind of resource this is: Company  
Inspo/Links:  
Any additional notes or information: Weekly marketing content focused on promoting senior care services tailored for Winnipeg clients.

attributes.name: Topic
attributes.description: The main focus, theme, or event identified in the text.

attributes.name: Type
attributes.description: The classification of the entry. Default is “Marketing/Promotion”.

attributes.name: Resources/Links
attributes.description: URLs or main resource links. Separate multiple with “; ”.

attributes.name: Please specify what kind of resource this is
attributes.description: Classify based on the link: “Company” if it contains “rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg”; otherwise “External” unless explicitly specified.

attributes.name: Inspo/Links
attributes.description: Links for inspiration, reference, or similar campaigns (if any). Separate with “; ”.

attributes.name: Any additional notes or information
attributes.description: Any extra context, ideas, or background info related to the entry.

parameters.text:
=You are an information extraction assistant for company resource planning.

You will receive input text through this variable:
{{ $json.resource }}

Example input:
Week of
Topic: Understanding Parkinson’s Disease: Essential Care Tips for Families and Caregivers
URL: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/resources/understanding-parkinsons-disease-care-tips/

Your task:

Extract and structure the resource information into the following variables:

Topic

Type

Resources/Links

Please specify what kind of resource this is

Inspo/Links

Any additional notes or information

Always set Type to “Company Resource”.

Use the Topic line as the primary subject. If multiple topics appear, select the clearest main one.

For Resources/Links:

Extract any URLs found in the text.

Separate multiple links with “; ”.

For Please specify what kind of resource this is:

If the URL includes /resources/, classify it as “Educational / Blog”.

If the URL includes /guides/ or downloadable content, classify it as “Guide”.

Otherwise, classify it as “Company Resource”.

For Inspo/Links:

Leave blank unless the text explicitly references inspiration sources or related materials.

Place any extra context, seasonal relevance, or usage ideas under Any additional notes or information.

If no data exists for a variable, leave it blank (do not write “N/A”).

Output only the extracted fields, each on its own line, matching the variable names exactly.

attributes.name: Topic
attributes.description: The main educational theme or subject of the company resource.

attributes.name: Type
attributes.description: The classification of the entry. Always “Company Resource”.

attributes.name: Resources/Links
attributes.description: Direct URL(s) to the resource. Separate multiple links with “; ”.

attributes.name: Please specify what kind of resource this is
attributes.description: The format of the resource (e.g., Educational / Blog, Guide, Company Resource).

attributes.name: Inspo/Links
attributes.description: Optional inspiration or related reference links, if explicitly mentioned.

attributes.name: Any additional notes or information
attributes.description: Extra context such as intended audience, caregiver focus, or seasonal relevance.
